title: crotalaria serpentinicola
intro: Crotalaria serpentinicola is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain toxic compounds called pyrrolizidine alkaloids, but in small amounts, it has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, and it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Crotalaria serpentinicola is often used in combination with other plants to create remedies for various ailments, and it is typically consumed as a tea or tincture.
